key_departures.impact: Two-time All-Pro CB traded to the Rams for the 29th overall pick, 2026 5th, 2026 6th, and 2027 3rd-round pick. Was the unquestioned CB1 and best player in the secondary. Rams immediately signed him to a 4-year, $124M extension ($31M AAV), confirming his elite tier. Massive hole created at CB1.

Starting safety signed a 3-year, $40.25M deal with Bengals. Had 47 starts over three seasons in KC. Combined with McDuffie trade, Chiefs lost three secondary starters in one week. Critical safety need created.

Starting CB2 signed a 3-year, $51M deal with the Rams — reuniting with former teammate McDuffie. Was a key contributor in 2025 with 2 INTs, 6 PDs. Departure further decimates the CB room alongside the McDuffie trade.
Third cornerback from the 2022 draft class to depart. Signed a two-year deal in Tennessee. Depth loss compounds the already critical CB need.

Signed a 3-year, $24.75M deal ($12.4M guaranteed) with Washington. Was a key blitzer and versatile LB who had grown into a significant role. Loss hurts depth behind Nick Bolton and Drue Tranquill, though the position was not a critical need given existing roster.

Signed a 1-year deal (up to $7M) with Washington. Was primarily a situational pass rusher on 55% of defensive snaps. 28 tackles, 3.5 sacks in 2025. Depth loss at EDGE behind Chris Jones, compounds already existing pass rush need.
Signed a 1-year deal with Detroit. Was the nominal starter but had zero 20+ yard carries in 2025. Departure is a net positive given the Walker signing — this was expected and planned.

Backup QB signed a 1-year, $8.25M deal with Arizona. Filled in briefly after Mahomes' ACL tear before suffering his own knee injury. Chiefs now lack a proven backup QB with Mahomes recovering from ACL surgery.

Released March 4 to clear $20M in cap space. Was the starting RT for 3 years on a 4-year, $80M deal. Blocking had fallen off in 2025 and persistent penalty issues made this a justified cut. Creates a significant RT need.
Released Feb. 23 to clear ~$9M in cap space. Had a down year in 2025. Adds to the EDGE depth need but was not a high-production player.
Long-tenured rotational DT signed with the Colts. Was the primary IDL depth behind Chris Jones. Departure partially addressed by Tonga signing.
Led Chiefs in rushing in 2025 with 611 yards and 8 TDs but on aging legs at 31. Departure expected with Walker signing. Chiefs may explore a short-yardage specialist role but is not a critical need.

signings.analysis: Reigning Super Bowl MVP signed the richest RB free agent deal in NFL history at a base $43.05M with up to $45M. $28.7M fully guaranteed. Effectively a 2-year commitment with a team option in Year 3. Obliterates the RB need — Walker forced missed tackles on 30.2% of his touches (3rd-highest rate in NFL, min. 150 touches) and had 10 rushes of 20+ yards in 2025. RB drops from a tier-1 critical need to a tier-5 non-need. No draft capital needed at RB.

Three-year deal with $15M guaranteed partially replaces Bryan Cook's departure. At $8.25M AAV, this is mid-starter tier for S. Gilman recorded 90 tackles, 9 PBUs, 2 fumble recoveries and a TD in 2025 after joining the Ravens mid-season. He'll play alongside Chamarri Conner and Jaden Hicks. However, Gilman is 28, the deal does not carry elite guarantees, and the overall safety room is now thinner than it was in 2025. Safety remains a tier-2 draft need — the Chiefs need youth and long-term solutions there.

Three-year, $21M deal with $14M guaranteed — a real multi-year investment at IDL. Provides run-stuffing and nose tackle depth alongside Chris Jones. Had a career year in 2025 (24 tackles, 2 TFLs, 2 QB hits, 2 PDs). Jones reacted positively. Addresses the void left by Nnadi's departure, but Jones is still the only elite IDL. IDL goes from a tier-1 depth need to tier-3 — still worth a mid-round draft pick for long-term development, but the starter problem is solved.

One-year deal worth up to $15M with incentives. Kelce led the team in 2025 with 76 receptions, 851 yards, 5 TDs, 108 targets. His return provides critical Mahomes continuity but the 1-year structure signals this is a bridge, not a long-term solution. TE remains a tier-2/3 draft need for the future — Chiefs need to find Kelce's eventual successor, likely through the draft at rounds 3-5.

Two-year deal worth up to $14M (base $11M per Spotrac; guarantees TBD, likely incentive-heavy). Thornton was the team's top deep threat in 2025 with 438 yards (23.1 ypc, best in NFL among qualifiers) and 3 TDs. Re-signing him preserves the WR3 role. However, at $5.5M AAV, this is depth/WR3 money and the contract structure has minimal guaranteed base — more of a prove-it deal. WR room still has Rice (contract year), Worthy (returning from shoulder surgery), and Royals. WR remains a tier-2 draft need, particularly with Rice's future uncertain.

One-year deal (financial terms not yet confirmed per OTC/Spotrac). Missed ALL of 2025 with a torn ACL suffered in Dolphins training camp. Was a projected starting nickel CB with 38 career starts and versatility to play inside and outside. This is a prove-it/discount signing on a team-friendly contract due to the injury. He could win the nickel CB job but there is significant health risk and zero multi-year commitment. CB remains a tier-1 draft need — Chiefs lost McDuffie, Watson, and Williams and have only Nohl Williams, Kristian Fulton (release candidate), Kohou (health unknown), and Chamarri Conner remaining. Must address CB heavily in the draft.

One-year depth signing behind Walker. Financial terms not yet confirmed. Provides competition at RB2/3 behind Walker, Brashard Smith, and ShunDerrick Powell. Low-impact; no effect on draft need picture at RB.

Returned to KC on a below-RFA-tender deal after the Chiefs declined to tender him the $3.52M qualifying offer. Reports indicate he turned down other teams to return at a lower price. Provides depth IOL competition but is not a starter. IOL need is unchanged — Humphrey, Smith, and Suamataia form the starting trio; interior line depth remains a consideration.
Special teams/depth LB retained. Four-year presence on the roster primarily as a special teams contributor. No impact on positional need.
ERFA tender at $1.075M non-guaranteed. Primary return specialist role. Will face competition for the role. No impact on WR need picture.
ERFA tender retains the punter but KC may bring in competition. Minimal roster impact.
12th season with the Chiefs. Special teams continuity retained. No positional impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Chiefs lost THREE cornerbacks from the starting/rotation group: McDuffie (traded, All-Pro), Watson (UFA to Rams, $51M deal), and Joshua Williams (UFA to Titans). Kohou (1-year, torn ACL rehab) is the only meaningful FA addition. Remaining CBs are Nohl Williams (promising rookie), Kristian Fulton (release candidate), Chamarri Conner, and young unproven players. This is the single biggest roster need going into the draft. Must address CB in Rounds 1-3 — potentially as early as pick No. 9 or No. 29.

Lost Bryan Cook (3yr/$40.25M to Bengals) but added Alohi Gilman (3yr/$24.75M/$15M gtd). Gilman is a legitimate starter but slightly below Cook's level. Room also includes Chamarri Conner and Jaden Hicks. Need is partially addressed but not solved long-term — Gilman is 28 and on a bridge-length deal. Still a Tier 2 need for mid-round draft capital, especially given the overall secondary renovation.

Jawaan Taylor was cut (saves $20M, $7.4M dead cap). No OT was signed in free agency. Jaylon Moore is the only current option at RT. The Chiefs have picks No. 9 and No. 29 in Round 1 — offensive tackle (Francis Mauigoa, Spencer Fano, etc.) must be addressed in Rounds 1-2. Starting RT is a genuine hole.

Gardner Minshew departed (1yr/$8.25M to Cardinals). Mahomes is recovering from a torn ACL with Week 1 availability uncertain. Chiefs currently have no proven backup QB on the roster. Need a legitimate bridge QB — either via free agency (second wave) or a veteran trade — as well as potentially drafting a developmental QB. Escalated from depth need to genuine concern.

Kenneth Walker III signing (3yr/$43.05M, $28.7M gtd) is the richest RB free agent deal in NFL history. Walker is the Super Bowl MVP, age 25, locked in through at least 2027 with full guarantees. RB is no longer a draft need. Emari Demercado adds depth. Do not spend draft capital here.

Khyiris Tonga (3yr/$21M, $14M gtd) fills the Nnadi void as a legitimate nose tackle alongside Chris Jones. Three-year, $14M guaranteed signals a real investment. IDL drops from a priority need to a developmental/depth consideration. A Day 3 rotational IDL pick is fine but no longer urgent.

Chiefs lost both Charles Omenihu (Commanders, $7M) and Mike Danna (cut). No EDGE was signed in free agency. George Karlaftis remains as the primary pass rusher alongside Chris Jones. EDGE depth is thin. Still a Tier 2 need — should address in Rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Thornton re-signed (2yr/$11M), but this is a depth-tier deal for a WR3. Worthy (shoulder rehab), Rice (contract year, status uncertain), and Royals are the key pieces. Hollywood Brown and JuJu have departed. WR room is thin beyond Rice and Worthy. Need a true WR2 complement — likely a Rounds 2-3 draft target.

Kelce's return (1yr/$12M) stabilizes the position for 2026, but his age (36) and 1-year deal mean TE succession planning is still critical. Noah Gray remains as TE2 but offers limited upside. Chiefs should draft a TE in Rounds 3-5 to begin developing Kelce's long-term replacement.

Leo Chenal departed (3yr/$24.75M to Commanders) but Kansas City still has Nick Bolton and Drue Tranquill under contract. Tranquill is a potential cap casualty but Bolton anchors the unit. LB need is unchanged — a developmental Day 3 pick would help offset Chenal's departure.

Caliendo returned on a below-market 1-year deal. Starting trio of Humphrey, T. Smith, and Suamataia is elite and intact. Caliendo provides depth. IOL is a depth need only — a Day 3 competition piece could help but this is not a high-priority position.

key_additions.headline: Super Bowl LX MVP and most explosive RB in free agency eliminates KC's longest-standing offensive weakness
Veteran starter partially patches secondary after Bryan Cook's departure to Cincinnati
Run-stuffing nose tackle brings legitimate IDL depth alongside Chris Jones for first time in years
Kelce returns for 14th season, providing critical Mahomes continuity atop the offense
Career-year deep threat retained to anchor a WR room that still needs significant reinforcement
Former Dolphins nickel starter (missed all 2025 with ACL) takes a flier on reclaiming starting role in KC's depleted secondary

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded; signed 4yr/$124M ($31M AAV) extension with Rams
key_losses.headline: Two-time All-Pro CB1 dealt for pick No. 29, three additional picks — historic secondary teardown begins
Starting safety returns home to Cincinnati, leaving the Chiefs' backend with major vacancy
CB2 reunites with McDuffie in LA — Chiefs lose both starting corners in one week
Starting RT cut to clear $20M in cap space, leaving a glaring vacancy at right tackle heading into the draft
Young versatile LB and blitz specialist departs, continuing the exodus of KC's 2022 draft class
Backup QB departs as Mahomes rehabs torn ACL — KC now lacks a proven insurance policy at quarterback
